name: The Last Engineer
description: One of the twelve SCAMP engineers integrated with the crystal lattice three hundred years ago and has been coherent inside it ever since. The Ninth, in the Hall of Rememberers, maintains access to the lattice's memory. The engineer is still there. Still waiting.
accept_text: 'The Ninth.' Foss says it immediately. 'The Hall of Rememberers. The Elder mind in the fourth matrix — it has maintained contact with the lattice's memory since the Cascade. If the engineer's signature is in the lattice, and the Ninth is also in the lattice, they know each other.' She looks at the sealed case. 'I want to come with you. I want to hear what the engineer says, if it speaks.' She pauses. 'But I'm not supposed to be in the Hall — my access was revoked after I filed a report that asked whether the crystal matrices counted as living subjects under research ethics guidelines.' She looks at you with the expression of someone who does not regret the report but does regret the consequence. 'So you'll have to go without me. Take the case. Show the Ninth the commissioning document — not the confessions, those are private. The document that names the authority.' She meets your eyes. 'The Ninth will recognize the authority. It was alive when this happened. Whatever it knows — whatever the engineer has been waiting to say — ask. Ask everything.' She is quiet. 'If the engineer has been in the lattice for three hundred years holding a confession and waiting for someone to come, the least we can do is arrive.'
complete_text: The Ninth goes very still when you show it the commissioning document. The crystal matrix brightens and dims in a sequence that you have learned to recognize as the Elder equivalent of someone closing their eyes. Then it speaks — not about the document but to something else in the lattice, something you cannot perceive directly. A longer pause. Then, through the Ninth, something addresses you directly. The resonance is different from the Ninth's — older in a different way, human in a different way, carrying the specific weight of three hundred years of holding a single thought and waiting to release it. It says: the authority who commissioned the Gray Wastes was not Terran or Aetherian. It was the organization that preceded both governments — the Silent, under a different name, before the war gave them purpose. They saw the war coming. They commissioned the permanent no-man's-land to ensure it would never resolve. The twelve engineers were tools for a plan that predated the conflict by a decade. The engineer integrated with the lattice to preserve the evidence inside something the Silent could not destroy. To wait. To tell. The commissioning document is the proof, but the testimony is the meaning. Three hundred years of meaning, delivered in the space of a held breath. The Ninth, afterward, says only: 'It has been waiting a long time to tell someone that. It will rest now.' The matrix dims slightly. Something that has been holding itself open for three centuries lets itself close.
